Current interests
I am particularly interested in the integration of survey data, administrative records, geospatial information, and statistical models to improve the production of social indicators at detailed geographic levels.

title: Welcome to my blog
text:
This is my first blog post.
I will use this space to share short notes, examples, tutorials, and reflections on household surveys, official statistics, small area estimation, poverty measurement, and data analysis with R.

title: Books
text: I am a passionate reseracher on sampling methods, household surveys, analysis of complex survey data. So, I have published some books related to those matters.

title: Books
text:
Survey Sampling
This is the place to start if you want to learn about sampling methods and how to apply them in a real life context with the R software.

title: Books
text:
Household Surveys Analysis with R
If you are analysing some household survey data, the R software is the right tool to use.

text:

Written in Spanish, this book deals with the exact topics that a sampling methodologist is faced in a dayly basis.

title: Andrés Gutiérrez
text:
I am a statistician specializing in household surveys, survey sampling and small area estimation. Since 2017, I have served as Regional Adviser on Social Statistics at the United Nations Economic Commission for Latin America and the Caribbean (UN ECLAC), where I provide technical assistance to national statistical offices across the region.
My work focuses on developing and applying rigorous statistical methods to improve the design, analysis, and use of household surveys, strengthen official statistics, and support evidence-based public policy, with particular emphasis on social indicators. I am an Elected Member of the International Statistical Institute and a former Vice-President of the International Association of Survey Statisticians.
